growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

up.units: % of initial headspace CO consumed after 4 days
up.method: GC headspace (HP 6890); CO consumption coupled to growth, stopped at stationary phase

sec.method: GC/analytical; major product; partly re-consumed after several days (net yield, not per-time rate)
analytical; not detected through 7 days (no acetone pathway in genome)
GC headspace; CO2 produced coupled to CO consumption/growth
GC headspace; only traces detected at stationary phase

growth_records.cite: Guillaume Bruant et al. Genomic Analysis of Carbon Monoxide Utilization and Butanol Production by Clostridium carboxidivorans Strain P7T. PLoS ONE (2010). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0013033. [PMC2946384]
growth_records.snippet: Acetate was the major product with 56.58 mmol produced per mol of CO consumed, followed by ethanol (38.21 mmol produced per mol of CO consumed), butyrate (36.45 mmol produced per mol of CO consumed) and butanol (8.91 mmol produced per mol o
growth_records.notes: Autotrophic syngas (100% CO) fermentation via Wood-Ljungdahl. Product yields reported per mol CO consumed (mmol/mol CO), NOT as per-biomass per-time rates, so placed in yields.product_yields and secretion_rates with those units (is_experimental true). CO uptake reported only as 73% of initial headspace CO consumed after 4 days (no gDW normalization -> no mmol/gDW/h derivable). Growth non-exponential: 24 h lag then OD600 plateau 0.77 at day 4; no initial OD or doubling time given, so mu not computable. Acetate and (to a lesser degree) butyrate were re-consumed after several days (net vs gross production).
